body:
**Actionable comments posted: 11** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> .github/workflows/release-please.yml (1)
> > `3-6`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_ > > **Non-standard trigger pattern for release-please workflow.** > > The workflow uses `pull_request: types: [closed]` with a merged check instead of the standard `push` trigger on the main branch. This pattern is non-standard for release-please workflows and could cause issues: > > - The checkout context may not be at the main branch tip > - Standard release-please workflows trigger on `push` to main for clearer intent > - The current pattern adds unnecessary complexity with the merged condition check > > > > >
> 🔄 Recommended fix to use standard trigger pattern > > ```diff > on: > - pull_request: > - types: [closed] > + push: > + branches: > + - main > workflow_dispatch: > ``` > > And simplify the condition: > > ```diff > - if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || (github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.merged == true) }} > + # Runs on manual trigger or push to main > ``` > >
> > As per coding guidelines: "Use `release-please` GitHub workflow for automated semantic versioning and release generation on the main branch" > > > Also applies to: 18-18 > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In @.github/workflows/release-please.yml around lines 3 - 6, Update the workflow > trigger from the non-standard pull_request closed pattern to the standard > release-please trigger: replace the "on: pull_request: types: [closed]" block > with "on: push: branches: [main]" (keep "workflow_dispatch" if manual runs are > desired) and remove any merged-check condition elsewhere in the job that depends > on pull_request context so the release-please run operates on the main branch > tip. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
.github/workflows/docs-deploy.yml (1)
`7-10`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Remove the duplicate docs path entry.** Line 10 repeats `docs/**`, which is redundant in `on.push.paths`. Keeping a single entry makes trigger rules clearer.
Proposed cleanup ```diff paths: - 'docs/**' - 'mkdocs.yml' - 'README.md' - - 'docs/**' ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In @.github/workflows/docs-deploy.yml around lines 7 - 10, The on.push.paths array contains a duplicated entry 'docs/**' causing redundancy; edit the workflow definition so that the 'on.push.paths' list (the paths array shown in the diff) contains only one 'docs/**' entry by removing the repeated line, ensuring the remaining entries ('mkdocs.yml', 'README.md', and 'docs/**') are unique and preserve order. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In @.github/workflows/docker-release.yml: - Around line 13-16: The job-level if expression uses the unsupported secrets context; define a workflow-level variable and switch the conditional to use vars instead. Add a top-level vars entry named DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR that pulls from the secret (e.g. DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR }}), then update the jobs.build.if expression from github.event.release.author.login == (secrets.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR || github.repository_owner) to github.event.release.author.login == (vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR || github.repository_owner) so the job-level condition validates correctly. In @.github/workflows/release-please.yml: - Around line 24-29: Update the documentation comment for the release-please action to correct the input name: replace the example `create-release: false` with the valid `skip-github-release: true` setting for `release-please-action@v3` so the workflow generates a release PR but does not automatically create a GitHub release; ensure the comment mentions `skip-github-release` explicitly and keeps the context about preventing automatic publishes while still generating release PRs. In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/_helpers.tpl`: - Line 24: The helper currently forces callers to compute indentation by using `toYaml $labels | nindent 0`; update the helper in _helpers.tpl to emit the labels without any nindent so callers can consistently apply indentation (e.g., change to `toYaml $labels` output) and then update templates that include this helper to use a consistent caller-side `nindent` (for example `nindent 4` under `metadata:`). Locate the helper that references `$labels` and `toYaml` and remove the `nindent 0` pipeline, then ensure all call sites apply a consistent `nindent` (or add a short comment in the helper describing the expected caller indentation). In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/db-pvc.yaml`: - Around line 6-7: The labels block under metadata is misindented because the template call uses nindent 2; update the indentation so label keys are indented four spaces from metadata by changing the template invocation to use nindent 4 (i.e., adjust the include "social-media-reuploader.labels" . | nindent 2 usage to nindent 4) so the generated YAML under metadata: is valid. In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/deployment.yaml`: - Around line 11-15: The Deployment's .spec.selector currently only matches app.kubernetes.io/instance which is too broad; change the selector to match the exact labels used on the Pod template so the Deployment only selects its own pods. Replace the single-key selector with the full set of labels used in template.metadata.labels (reuse the existing include "social-media-reuploader.labels" or create a dedicated include like "social-media-reuploader.selectorLabels") and ensure .spec.selector.matchLabels exactly equals that same label set (preserve Helm templating and correct indentation). In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/gui-deployment.yaml`: - Around line 12-16: The Deployment's selector is too broad because it only uses app.kubernetes.io/instance and can match non-GUI pods; update the selector.matchLabels to include a GUI-specific label present in the pod template (e.g. app.kubernetes.io/component: gui or a dedicated label from the included labels template) and ensure the same label is present under template.metadata.labels (see selector.matchLabels, template.metadata.labels and the include "social-media-reuploader.labels"); this guarantees the Deployment only selects GUI pods and prevents collision with the main deployment. In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/httproute.yaml`: - Around line 6-7: The labels block under metadata is mis-indented because the template uses {{ include "social-media-reuploader.labels" . | nindent 2 }} which only indents 2 spaces; change the indentation to 4 spaces so the generated label keys align under metadata (replace nindent 2 with nindent 4 for the include "social-media-reuploader.labels" invocation). In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/secret.yaml`: - Around line 2-3: The fail message in the template is misleading: when secret.create is true you still must supply env.BOT_TOKEN, but the message suggests setting secret.name instead. Update the fail text used where the template checks "if not .Values.env.BOT_TOKEN" to clearly state the remediation: either provide env.BOT_TOKEN (e.g. --set env.BOT_TOKEN=...) when using secret.create=true, or set secret.create=false and supply an existing secret via secret.name; reference the existing symbols "secret.create", "env.BOT_TOKEN", and "secret.name" in the message so users know both valid options. In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/serviceaccount.yaml`: - Around line 6-7: labels block in serviceaccount.yaml is misindented because the template uses nindent 2; change the indentation so label keys are indented 4 spaces under metadata by replacing the current include call using nindent 2 with the correct indentation helper (use nindent 4) for the include "social-media-reuploader.labels" invocation so that the labels: entries are valid YAML under metadata. In `@src/downloader.py`: - Around line 241-246: The nested if checking yt_dlp_bin inside the outer if yt_dlp_bin block is redundant and leaves the else branch unreachable; simplify the logic in downloader.py by removing the inner conditional and directly set recode_cmd to use yt_dlp_bin when building the command (e.g., construct recode_cmd = [yt_dlp_bin, "--no-playlist", "--recode-video", "mp4", "-o", out_template, url]) so the variable recode_cmd is correctly assigned using yt_dlp_bin, and delete the unreachable else branch. - Around line 126-128: The code currently builds trans_path from latest which can equal the source when latest already ends with .mp4, causing ffmpeg -y to truncate the source; change the logic that computes trans_path (used in ffmpeg_cmd) to always use a distinct temporary output filename (e.g., append a unique suffix or use a tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile / mkstemp) and run ffmpeg to write to that temp file, then atomically move/rename the temp to the final target only after ffmpeg exits successfully; ensure you reference the same variables (latest, trans_path, ffmpeg_cmd) when implementing the temp path and also add cleanup of the temp file on failure. --- Outside diff comments: In @.github/workflows/release-please.yml: - Around line 3-6: Update the workflow trigger from the non-standard pull_request closed pattern to the standard release-please trigger: replace the "on: pull_request: types: [closed]" block with "on: push: branches: [main]" (keep "workflow_dispatch" if manual runs are desired) and remove any merged-check condition elsewhere in the job that depends on pull_request context so the release-please run operates on the main branch tip. --- Nitpick comments: In @.github/workflows/docs-deploy.yml: - Around line 7-10: The on.push.paths array contains a duplicated entry 'docs/**' causing redundancy; edit the workflow definition so that the 'on.push.paths' list (the paths array shown in the diff) contains only one 'docs/**' entry by removing the repeated line, ensuring the remaining entries ('mkdocs.yml', 'README.md', and 'docs/**') are unique and preserve order. ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) ❌ Autofix failed (check again to retry) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes

**Actionable comments posted: 2**
♻️ Duplicate comments (1)
.github/workflows/docker-release.yml (1)
`13-17`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _⚡ Quick win_ **Use `vars` (not `secrets`) in job-level `if`.** Line 17 references `secrets.*` in `jobs.build.if`, which is not supported at job scope and can break workflow validation/execution.
Proposed fix ```diff - if: ${{ github.event.release.author.login == (secrets.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR || github.repository_owner) || github.event.release.author.login == 'github-actions' || github.event.release.author.login == 'github-actions[bot]' }} + if: ${{ github.event.release.author.login == (vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR || github.repository_owner) || github.event.release.author.login == 'github-actions' || github.event.release.author.login == 'github-actions[bot]' }} ```
```shell #!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail file=".github/workflows/docker-release.yml" echo "Inspecting job-level if:" nl -ba "$file" | sed -n '12,20p' echo echo "Checking for unsupported secrets context in job-level if (expect: no matches):" rg -nP '^\s*if:\s*\$\{\{[^}]*\bsecrets\.' "$file" || true echo echo "If actionlint is available, run it (expect: no context-availability error):" if command -v actionlint >/dev/null 2>&1; then actionlint "$file" else echo "actionlint not installed in this environment." fi ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In @.github/workflows/docker-release.yml around lines 13 - 17, The job-level conditional (jobs.build.if) uses secrets.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR which is unsupported at job scope; update the if expression to reference vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR instead (i.e., change secrets.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR to vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR) in the condition that checks github.event.release.author.login (and keep the existing checks for 'github-actions' and 'github-actions[bot]'); alternatively, move the check to a step-level if or set an env/outputs value and reference that, but the simplest fix is replacing secrets.* with vars.* in the jobs.build.if expression. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/service.yaml`: - Around line 12-13: The Service selector currently only uses app.kubernetes.io/instance and is too broad; update the Service's selector to include workload-specific labels (for example add app.kubernetes.io/name and/or app.kubernetes.io/component) so it uniquely targets the intended pods, and then update the target workload's podTemplate metadata.labels (Deployment/StatefulSet/Pod spec) to include the exact same label keys/values so the Service selector and pod labels match precisely. In `@src/downloader.py`: - Around line 170-172: The remux path may collide with the source when latest already ends with .mp4; change the remux_path creation in src/downloader.py so it always uses a distinct temporary filename (e.g., append a unique suffix or use a temp file in dest_dir) instead of os.path.splitext(base) + ".mp4"; ensure the ffmpeg_cmd still writes to that temp remux file and, after successful remux, rename/move the temp file to the final name or replace the original safely; update any references to remux_path, ffmpeg_cmd and the code path that checks format_name to use the temp filename to avoid in-place overwrites. --- Duplicate comments: In @.github/workflows/docker-release.yml: - Around line 13-17: The job-level conditional (jobs.build.if) uses secrets.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR which is unsupported at job scope; update the if expression to reference vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR instead (i.e., change secrets.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR to vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR) in the condition that checks github.event.release.author.login (and keep the existing checks for 'github-actions' and 'github-actions[bot]'); alternatively, move the check to a step-level if or set an env/outputs value and reference that, but the simplest fix is replacing secrets.* with vars.* in the jobs.build.if expression. ```

**Actionable comments posted: 4**
🧹 Nitpick comments (2)
src/downloader.py (2)
`263-316`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Likely dead/duplicate path — and `ffmpeg_bin or "ffmpeg"` can shell out to a missing binary.** This block was the original audio-only wrapper and is now reachable only if the new block at 193–238 left `ext` in `audio_exts` (i.e. either the new ffmpeg attempt failed or the yt-dlp recode fallback produced audio). In the ffmpeg-failed case, re-running the same command isn't going to help; in the no-ffmpeg path, `ffmpeg_bin` is `None` and `ffmpeg_bin or "ffmpeg"` falls back to invoking a literal `"ffmpeg"` that isn't on `PATH`, which raises `FileNotFoundError` and is only swallowed by the outer `except Exception`. Recommend removing this block (and folding any logging you want to keep — e.g. stdout/stderr capture — into the new wrapper above) once the consolidation in the previous comment is done.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@src/downloader.py` around lines 263 - 316, This audio-only ffmpeg wrapper block is now redundant and unsafe: remove the entire try/except that creates mp4_path, builds ffmpeg_cmd using (ffmpeg_bin or "ffmpeg"), launches proc3, and handles stdout/stderr/timeout (the code that checks proc3.returncode and sets latest/ext to mp4), because it duplicates the new wrapper and can raise FileNotFoundError when ffmpeg_bin is None; instead, fold any useful logging (decoded stdout/stderr, timeout warnings, and the final file stat/logging) into the consolidated wrapper that runs earlier (the code that handles ffmpeg invocation for audio/video and updates latest/ext), ensuring all ffmpeg invocations use the same ffmpeg_bin handling and centralized error/timeout handling rather than this duplicate block. ```
--- `193-238`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Wrapper logic is now duplicated with the legacy block below.** This new audio-only ffmpeg wrapper (lines 193–238) does essentially the same work as the pre-existing block at lines 262–316: same `_wrapped.mp4` target, same ffmpeg arguments. After this block runs successfully, `ext` becomes `"mp4"` and the second block is skipped; if it fails, re-running the exact same ffmpeg invocation a few lines later is unlikely to succeed and just produces noisier logs. Consider consolidating both audio-only wrap attempts into a single helper and dropping the legacy block at 262–316 to avoid drift between the two copies.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@src/downloader.py` around lines 193 - 238, The audio-only ffmpeg wrapper logic is duplicated; consolidate it by extracting a helper (e.g., wrap_audio_with_black_video) that takes latest, dest_dir, ffmpeg_bin, timeout and returns the new path/extension/size or raises an error, then replace both blocks (the current one using variables latest, ext, mp4_path, ffmpeg_cmd, proc3, timeout and the legacy block at lines 262–316) with a single call to that helper; remove the legacy block to avoid duplicate ffmpeg invocation and ensure the helper logs errors consistently and updates latest/ext/size when successful. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In @.github/workflows/docker-release.yml: - Around line 11-12: Remove the invalid top-level "vars:" block and any inline definition of DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR from the workflow; instead document/update the comment near the existing DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR usage to say this value must be created as a repository Actions variable (Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → Variables) named DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR, and keep using the variable reference ${{ vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR }} (or switch to ${{ secrets.NAME }} if you intend to store it as a secret). - Line 20: Remove the invalid top-level vars block and fix the job "if" condition by comparing github.event.release.author.login against each allowed author string individually (use OR between comparisons) instead of using a logical OR inside the comparison; specifically update the existing if expression that references github.event.release.author.login to check equality versus vars.DOCKER_RELEASE_ALLOWED_AUTHOR, github.repository_owner, 'github-actions', and 'github-actions[bot]' as separate comparisons. Also update the referenced actions docker/setup-qemu-action and docker/login-action from v2 to v3 to use the current action versions. In `@charts/social-media-reuploader/templates/deployment.yaml`: - Around line 16-17: The labels block in templates/deployment.yaml is misindented so entries under the pod template "labels:" key are at the same level; fix by indenting the label entries two more spaces than the "labels:" key—change the include call from nindent 6 to nindent 8 for {{ include "social-media-reuploader.labels" . }} and indent the literal label line "app.kubernetes.io/component: bot" so both label entries are nested one level deeper than the "labels:" key. In `@src/downloader.py`: - Around line 166-191: The remux block under the else: branch is mis-indented causing an IndentationError; move the line with if fmt and "mp4" not in fmt: to be directly inside that else: body and then nest the entire try/except block (the try:, the ffmpeg invocation using ffmpeg_cmd, proc5, asyncio.wait_for and the corresponding except/else handling that sets latest/remux_path/ext/size) inside that if; ensure the matching except Exception: that logs "Remux failed" is aligned with the try so the try/except pair are at the same indentation level and the if/try are consistently indented within the else. --- Nitpick comments: In `@src/downloader.py`: - Around line 263-316: This audio-only ffmpeg wrapper block is now redundant and unsafe: remove the entire try/except that creates mp4_path, builds ffmpeg_cmd using (ffmpeg_bin or "ffmpeg"), launches proc3, and handles stdout/stderr/timeout (the code that checks proc3.returncode and sets latest/ext to mp4), because it duplicates the new wrapper and can raise FileNotFoundError when ffmpeg_bin is None; instead, fold any useful logging (decoded stdout/stderr, timeout warnings, and the final file stat/logging) into the consolidated wrapper that runs earlier (the code that handles ffmpeg invocation for audio/video and updates latest/ext), ensuring all ffmpeg invocations use the same ffmpeg_bin handling and centralized error/timeout handling rather than this duplicate block. - Around line 193-238: The audio-only ffmpeg wrapper logic is duplicated; consolidate it by extracting a helper (e.g., wrap_audio_with_black_video) that takes latest, dest_dir, ffmpeg_bin, timeout and returns the new path/extension/size or raises an error, then replace both blocks (the current one using variables latest, ext, mp4_path, ffmpeg_cmd, proc3, timeout and the legacy block at lines 262–316) with a single call to that helper; remove the legacy block to avoid duplicate ffmpeg invocation and ensure the helper logs errors consistently and updates latest/ext/size when successful. ```
